popularStory.headline: Memorial
popularStory.description:
James Stewart, the Black Knight of Lorn
James Stewart, the Black Knight of Lorn (c.1383–c.1451) was a Scottish nobleman.

The Black Knight of Lorn was born at Innermeath, Scotland. His father, Sir Joh …

zh.lifeSummary: 當James Stewart Black Knight of Lorne於1399年出生在蘇格蘭阿吉爾時，他的父親John Stewart Laird of Lorn49歲，母親Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne38歲。他於1439年9月21日與Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他於1437年登記服兵役。他於1435年被列為master of the king's household - james i of scotland。他去世於1451年8月17日在英國蘇格蘭享年52歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James Stewart Black Knight of Lorne于1399年出生在苏格兰阿吉爾时，他的父亲John Stewart Laird of Lorn49岁，母亲Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne38岁。他于1439年9月21日与Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他于1437年登记服兵役。他于1435年被列为master of the king's household - james i of scotland。他去世于1451年8月17日在英国苏格兰享年52岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When James Stewart Black Knight of Lorne was born in 1399, in Argyll, Scotland, his father, John Stewart Laird of Lorn, was 49 and his mother, Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne, was 38. He married Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland on 21 September 1439. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He registered for military service in 1437. In 1435, his occupation is listed as master of the king's household - james i of scotland. He died on 17 August 1451, in Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 52, and was buried in Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Stewart Black Knight of Lorne est né en 1399, à Argyll, Écosse, son père, John Stewart Laird of Lorn, avait 49 ans et sa mère, Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland le 21 septembre 1439. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1437. En 1435, sa profession de son indiquée est master of the king's household - james i of scotland. Il est décédé le 17 août 1451, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als James Stewart Black Knight of Lorne 1399 in Argyll, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Stewart Laird of Lorn 49 und seine Mutter Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne 38. Er hat am 21. September 1439 Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er hat sich 1437 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1435 ist sein Beruf als master of the king's household - james i of scotland aufgeführt. Er starb am 17. August 1451 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James Stewart Black Knight of Lorne è nato il nel 1399 nel Argyll, Scotland, il suo padre, John Stewart Laird of Lorn, aveva 49 anni e il suo madre, Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne, aveva 38 anni. Lui sposato Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland il 21 settembre 1439. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1437. Nel 1435, la sua occupazione è indicata come master of the king's household - james i of scotland. Morì il 17 agosto 1451, nel Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolto a Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: James Stewart Black Knight of Lorneが1399年にスコットランドアルグイルで生まれた時，父John Stewart Laird of Lornは49歳，母Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorneは38歳であった。1439年9月21日にJoan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotlandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1437年に兵役登録をした。1435年，職業はmaster of the king's household - james i of scotlandと記録されている。彼は1451年8月17日にイギリススコットランドで52歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James Stewart Black Knight of Lorne(이)가 1399년 스컷랜드 아그이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Stewart Laird of Lorn(은)는 49세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne(은)는 38세였다. 그는 1439년 9월 21일 Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1437년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1435년, 그의 직업은 master of the king's household - james i of scotland이었다. 그는 1451년 8월 17일 영국 스코틀랜드에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando James Stewart Black Knight of Lorne nasceu em 1399, em Argyll, Scotland, seu pai, John Stewart Laird of Lorn, tinha 49 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne, tinha 38 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland em 21 de setembro de 1439. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1437. Em 1435, sua profissão aparece como master of the king's household - james i of scotland. Ele faleceu em 17 de agosto de 1451, em Escócia, United Kingdom, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Stewart Black Knight of Lorne родился в 1399 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия, его отцу по имени John Stewart Laird of Lorn было 49 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland 21 сентября 1439 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. В 1437 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1435 годуработал по профессии master of the king's household - james i of scotland. Он умер 17 августа 1451 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando James Stewart Black Knight of Lorne nació en 1399, en Argyll, Scotland, su papá, John Stewart Laird of Lorn, tenía 49 años y su mamá, Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Joan Beaufort Queen Consort of Scotland el 21 de septiembre de 1439. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1437. En 1435, su ocupación registrada era master of the king's household - james i of scotland. Murió el 17 de agosto de 1451, en Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Escocia, Reino Unido.
